title: sorbus aucuparia subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Sorbus aucuparia subsp. lanuginosa, also known as the Downy Rowan or Woolly Rowan, is a subspecies of the Rowan tree, a deciduous tree native to mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sorbus aucuparia subsp. lanuginosa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
